title: EGFR result review

examination.text: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.
examination.rationale: Supports evaluation of cause and kidney-function context for estimated GFR result review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Evaluation of cause and kidney-function context
exact_section.locator: Practice Point 1.1.4.1, Figure 8, Table 6, and Practice Point 1.2.1.1; summary PDF pages 34–35 and 61

investigations.text: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.
investigations.rationale: Supports CKD detection and chronicity review for estimated GFR result review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Detection of CKD and evaluation of chronicity
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 1.1.1.1–1.1.1.2 and 1.1.3.1–1.1.3.2; summary PDF pages 34 and 54–57

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.
follow_up.rationale: Supports clinician-led GFR and albuminuria monitoring for estimated GFR result review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
eGFR value, date, equation, creatinine, prior results, chronicity, ACR, acute context, medicines, cause, diagnosis, referral and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for estimated GFR result review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
